meta.title: Jobs & Work Basics
meta.description: Learn vocabulary about common jobs and simple work expressions.
meta.description_ko: 일반적인 직업과 간단한 직장 표현을 학습합니다.

explanation.en: A 'teacher' is a person who instructs students at a school.
These are common professional occupations.
'What do you do?' is a common way to ask someone's job or profession.
explanation.ko: 'What do you do?'는 직업을 묻는 일반적인 표현입니다.

questions.prompt: She is a _____. She works at a hospital. (그녀는 의사입니다. 병원에서 일합니다.)

explanation.en: A 'doctor' works at a hospital treating patients.
Use 'an' before jobs starting with a vowel sound: 'an engineer', 'an artist'.
explanation.ko: 모음 발음으로 시작하는 직업 앞에는 'an'을 씁니다: 'an engineer', 'an artist'.

explanation.en: A 'chef' is a professional cook, especially in a restaurant.
An 'office' is a room or building where people work, usually at desks.

questions.prompt: He _____ at a bank. He is a banker. (그는 은행에서 일합니다. 은행원입니다.)

explanation.en: 'Work at a place' describes where someone is employed.
A 'pilot' is the person who flies an aircraft.
'What do you do?' is the standard question for asking someone's job.
explanation.ko: 'What do you do?'는 직업을 묻는 표준 표현입니다.

explanation.en: Each job is associated with a specific workplace.
A 'lawyer' is a person qualified to give legal advice and represent people in court.
explanation.ko: 'lawyer'는 법률 자문을 제공하고 법정에서 사람들을 대리하는 변호사입니다.

questions.prompt: She is a _____. She takes photos for a living. (그녀는 사진작가입니다. 사진 찍는 일로 생계를 유지합니다.)

explanation.en: A 'photographer' is someone whose profession involves taking photographs.
Use 'a' before consonant sounds: 'a doctor', 'a teacher'. Use 'an' before vowels.
explanation.ko: 자음 발음 앞에는 'a'를 씁니다: 'a doctor'. 모음 발음 앞에는 'an'을 씁니다.

questions.answer: My father works in a factory.

explanation.en: 'Work in a + building type' vs 'work at a + specific place'. Both are common.
explanation.ko: 'work in a + 건물 유형' 또는 'work at a + 특정 장소' 모두 일반적으로 사용합니다.

explanation.en: A 'salary' is a fixed amount of money paid regularly for work.
explanation.ko: 'salary'는 일에 대해 정기적으로 지급되는 고정 금액인 월급/봉급입니다.

explanation.en: 'Accountant' starts with a vowel sound (a-), so 'an accountant' is correct.

questions.prompt: I go to _____ at 9 o'clock every morning. (나는 매일 아침 9시에 출근합니다.)

explanation.en: 'Go to work' means to travel to your place of employment.

questions.prompt: 다음 문장에서 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'He work as a designer.'

explanation.en: 3rd person singular 'He' requires 'works', not 'work'.
A 'part-time job' is a job where you work fewer hours than a full-time employee.
explanation.ko: 'part-time job'은 정규직보다 적은 시간을 일하는 아르바이트/시간제 근무입니다.

explanation.en: 'What does + person + do?' asks about someone else's job.
explanation.ko: 'What does + 사람 + do?'는 다른 사람의 직업을 묻는 표현입니다.

explanation.en: To 'retire' means to stop working permanently, usually at an older age.
explanation.ko: 'retire'는 보통 나이가 들어 영구적으로 일을 그만두는 은퇴입니다.

explanation.en: 'Occupation' is a formal word for 'job' or profession.

questions.prompt: She is a _____. She writes articles for a newspaper. (그녀는 기자입니다. 신문에 기사를 씁니다.)

explanation.en: A 'journalist' writes news articles for newspapers, magazines, or online media.
explanation.ko: 'journalist'는 신문, 잡지, 온라인 미디어에 기사를 쓰는 기자입니다.

questions.prompt: 다음 대화의 빈칸에 알맞은 것은? 'A: What do you do? B: I am _____ accountant. I work at a bank.'

explanation.en: 'Accountant' begins with a vowel sound, so 'an' is correct.
